empirical_cardinalities.note: Measured via 1% strided sampling program; orders scanned fully to ensure join overlap

join_order.probe: orders(filtered by o_orderdate<9204 via zone maps, ~7.35M rows)
join_order.rationale: Smallest filtered side (301K) builds hash set; orders probes — classic star schema dim-first ordering

join_order.build: qualifying_orders(~1461923 rows with orderdate, shippriority, revenue accumulator)
join_order.probe: lineitem(filtered by l_shipdate>9204 via zone maps, ~33M rows)
join_order.bloom_filter: applied before hash probe — 1.46M qualifying orderkeys in bloom; ~99% of lineitem probe attempts will miss, eliminating expensive cache-miss hash lookups
join_order.rationale: 1.46M build side fits in memory; 33M lineitem probe side screened by bloom filter for 2-5x speedup

filter_pushdown.implementation: load dict from c_mktsegment_dict.txt, find building_code (line index where value=='BUILDING'), filter uint8_t mktseg_byte == building_code
zone-map-guided: skip blocks where block_min >= 9204 (column sorted ascending, so once block_min>=9204 break); process remaining blocks row-by-row
probe custkey hash set built in step 1
zone-map-guided: column sorted ascending, binary search zone map for first block where block_max > 9204; scan from that row offset onward
bloom filter pre-check (L2-resident, 128KB for 1.46M keys at ~1% FP rate); eliminates ~99% of non-matching probe attempts before hash map lookup

data_structures.customer_filter_set: compact_hash_set(pre_sized=524288, open_addressing_linear_probe) — 301K int32_t custkeys; fits in ~2MB, well within L3
data_structures.orders_join_map: compact_hash_map(pre_sized=2097152, open_addressing_linear_probe) — maps int32_t orderkey -> struct{int32_t orderdate, int32_t shippriority, double revenue, int64_t count}; ~1.46M entries, ~50MB; NOT the pre-built orderkey_hash index (33.5M slots, cache-unfriendly and includes non-qualifying orders)
data_structures.bloom_filter_orderkeys: bloom_filter(128KB bitvector, 3 hash functions) — populated with 1.46M qualifying orderkeys; targets ~1% false positive rate; L2-resident for fast rejection of non-matching lineitem rows
data_structures.thread_local_agg: per_thread compact_hash_map(pre_sized=131072) — 77K estimated groups, aggregates revenue locally; merged after parallel phase
data_structures.topk_heap: std::priority_queue min-heap(size=10) — for ORDER BY revenue DESC, o_orderdate ASC LIMIT 10; O(N log 10) vs O(N log N) full sort

index_usage.layout: [uint32_t num_blocks=150] then [int32_t min, int32_t max, uint32_t start_row] x 150
index_usage.purpose: skip order blocks where block_min >= 9204; column sorted ascending so break on first block_min>=9204; eliminates ~51% of blocks from scan

index_usage.layout: [uint32_t num_blocks=600] then [int32_t min, int32_t max, uint32_t start_row] x 600
index_usage.purpose: binary search for first block where block_max > 9204; scan only from that row offset onward — eliminates ~45% of early lineitem blocks where all dates <= 1995-03-15

NOT USED — building a runtime hash map from only the 1.46M qualifying orders is more efficient: smaller table (~50MB vs ~256MB for full index), contains only qualifying entries so no post-probe filter needed, and avoids mmap of the 33.5M-slot index
NOT USED — building in-memory hash set from 301K BUILDING custkeys (after mktsegment filter) is more cache-efficient than probing the 4.2M-slot pre-built index

phase1_customer_scan.rationale: 1.5M rows, ~1.5MB total (uint8_t + int32_t) — fast enough single-threaded; avoids synchronization overhead for hash set build
phase1_customer_scan.output: shared flat hash set of 301K BUILDING custkeys

phase2_orders_scan.details: each thread processes morsels of qualifying order blocks (after zone-map skip); thread-local vectors of (orderkey, orderdate, shippriority) — merged sequentially into shared orders hash map after parallel scan
phase2_orders_scan.rationale: 1.46M qualifying orders fit into single hash map build post-merge; avoids contention during parallel probe

phase4_lineitem_scan_and_agg.details: each thread processes lineitem morsels from first qualifying block; bloom filter check (L2-resident, shared read-only), then hash map probe (shared read-only orders map), then accumulate into thread-local aggregation hash map
phase4_lineitem_scan_and_agg.merge_cost: low — 77K groups x 64 threads = 4.9M merge ops; sequential merge < 5ms

phase5_topk.details: iterate merged aggregation map (~77K entries), maintain min-heap of size 10 keyed by (revenue DESC, orderdate ASC)

column_load_order: customer/c_mktsegment.bin (filter column — enables custkey set build)
orders/o_orderdate.bin (filter column — apply date predicate in parallel with custkey probe)
orders/o_custkey.bin (join probe key — probe customer hash set)
orders/o_orderkey.bin (join key — build orders hash map key)
orders/o_shippriority.bin (payload — needed for qualifying orders only)
lineitem/l_shipdate.bin (filter column — apply date predicate first)
lineitem/l_orderkey.bin (join probe key — bloom check then hash probe)
lineitem/l_extendedprice.bin (payload — read only for qualifying rows)
lineitem/l_discount.bin + lineitem/l_discount_lookup.bin (payload — decode for qualifying rows)

selective_prefetch.orders: madvise MADV_WILLNEED only on blocks where block_min < 9204 (from orderdate_zonemap); stops at first block where block_min >= 9204
selective_prefetch.lineitem: madvise MADV_WILLNEED from first qualifying block offset onward (binary search on shipdate_zonemap for first block where block_max > 9204)

io_strategy.prefetch_note: Issue concurrent madvise calls across columns using OpenMP parallel section; kernel dispatches parallel HDD I/O requests

decimal_handling.l_discount: byte_pack encoded as uint8_t; decode via disc_lut[discount_code] from lineitem/l_discount_lookup.bin (256 doubles); compute (1.0 - disc_lut[code]) inline
decimal_handling.l_extendedprice: stored as native double; read directly, no decode needed

phase_breakdown.lineitem_parallel_scan_bloom_probe_agg: ~60-120ms (64 threads, ~33M qualifying rows, bloom eliminates ~99%, ~309K survive to hash probe + agg)

estimated_cost.cold_start_overhead: ~50-100ms additional for HDD I/O (zone-map-guided selective prefetch reduces page fault cost vs full column scan)
